title: Pleuropteropyrum japonicum
intro: Pleuropteropyrum japonicum, also known as Japanese bitter fumitory, is a perennial plant native to East Asia, characterized by its edible leaves and stems, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to regulate blood sugar levels and improve digestion. The bioactive constituents of Pleuropteropyrum japonicum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tea, and is also used to make capsules and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including diabetes and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Pleuropteropyrum japonicum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a perennial herb native to East Asia, specifically in Japan. The exact classification of Pleuropteropyrum japonicum is within the order Caryophyllales. This plant is further categorized as a genus Pleuropteropyrum in the family Caryophyllaceae. More specifically, its classification is in the family Caryophyllaceae, order Caryophyllales, class Magnoliopsida, and kingdom Plantae.
